title_en: Taiba Off to Strong Start at F-T July Yearling Sale
description: Spendthrift Farm's first-crop yearling sire Taiba has the top average among first-crop sires with two or more sold at Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale. The farm had four first-crop sires represented at the sale.

title_en: Donnacha O'Brien Looks for More Breeders' Cup Success
description: Donnacha O'Brien has the Breeders' Cup in mind for Eclipse Stakes (G1) runner-up A Boy Named Susie, who the trainer clarified has not been sold despite Australian media reports saying he had been bought for seven figures.

title_en: Knightsbridge Tests New Distance in Monmouth Cup
description: The $350,000 Monmouth Cup Stakes (G3) launches the graded action in the fourth race July 18 at Monmouth Park, with three-time graded stakes winner Knightsbridge stretching out to 1 1/8 miles to face Pegasus World Cup (G1) winner Skippylongstocking.

title_en: Brown Seeks 11th Victory in Saratoga's Grade 1 Diana
description: There's dominance. And then there's trainer Chad Brown in the Diana Stakes (G1T). Though Brown started training 19 years ago in 2007, he already owns 10 victories in the Diana.

title_en: Breeders' Cup Board of Directors Members Reelected
description: Barbara Banke, Walker Hancock, Gray Lyster, and Elliott Walden have been reelected to the Breeders' Cup's board of directors and will serve four-year terms.

title_en: Stonerside Co-Founder Janice McNair Dies at 89
description: Janice McNair, who founded Stonerside Stable with her husband Bob in 1984 before leaving the industry to launch the National Football League's Houston Texans, died July 14. She was 89.

title_en: IFHA Holds Climate Change and Sustainability Summit
description: Over 50 international industry leaders attend the IFHA's inaugural Climate Change and Sustainability Summit July 13-14 in Porto, Portugal.

title_en: Midwest-Based Owner/Breeder Otto Dies at 88
description: Horse owner and breeder Richard "Dick" Otto, whose stable campaigned graded stakes winners Summer Mis and Sir Anthony, dies at age 88.

title_en: Fasig-Tipton Releases Catalog for 105th Saratoga Sale
description: Fasig-Tipton has cataloged 230 selected yearlings for the 105th Saratoga Sale, to be held Aug. 10-11 in Saratoga Springs, N.Y. Sessions will begin each evening at 6:30 p.m. ET in the Humphrey S. Finney Sales Pavilion.

title_en: Alvarado, Saez Share Jockey of the Week Honors
description: Junior Alvarado and Luis Saez share Jockey of the Week honors after winning multiple stakes races July 6-12.

title_en: Cy Fair the Headliner in Coronation Cup
description: After missing a trip to Royal Ascot, Cy Fair is back in action July 18 against a quality field in the $225,000 Coronation Cup Stakes (G3T) at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Retired Ghostzapper Continues to Impress as a Sire
description: In this week's Porter on Pedigrees, Ghostzapper was represented during the July 11-12 weekend by similarly bred graded stakes winner Ole Crazy Bone and stakes winner Ghost Me.

title_en: No Takers to Keep Racing Going at Hawthorne
description: Last-ditch efforts to save racing at Hawthorne Race Course apparently have fallen short as the only bid at a bankruptcy auction came from a developer planning to demolish the track.

title_en: CTHS Ontario Details Enhanced Breeders' Incentives
description: The Canadian Thoroughbred Horse Society announces details of Ontario's Breeders' Awards and breeder incentive programs, made possible through the CA$5 million increase to Ontario Racing's Thoroughbred Improvement Program.

title_en: Taproot Secures $300K Taiba Colt, Two Fillies
description: Phil Hager of Taproot Bloodstock acquired three yearlings at Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale in Lexington, spending $515,000.

title_en: Former PTHA President DeBunda Dies at 83
description: Former Pennsylvania Thoroughbred Horsemen's Association president, Pennsylvania State Horse Racing commissioner, and Parx Racing Hall of Fame owner Salvatore DeBunda died July 9 at 83.

title_en: Our Moneyman Soars to $2M at F-T July HORA Sale
description: Agent Pedro Lanz buys the graded-placed, stakes-winning Our Moneyman for KAS Stables, which plans to keep the colt in the United States under the care of trainer Bret Calhoun. Lanz said the Sept. 19 Pennsylvania Derby (G1) is on their radar.

title_en: Napoleon Solo, Further Ado Headline Stellar Haskell
description: Napoleon Solo may be a classic winner, but trainer Chad Summers knows his 3-year-old colt will be facing his toughest test to date in the July 18 Haskell Stakes (G1) at Monmouth Park.

title_en: THA Announces New Strategic Initiative
description: The Thoroughbred Horsemen's Association has launched a new strategic initiative with the goal of positioning the organization to work for the future of Thoroughbred racing.

title_en: Girvin Filly at $450,000 Leads McCrocklin's $1.46M Haul
description: Pinhooker Tom McCrocklin emerges as the leading yearling buyer July 14 during The July Sale at Fasig-Tipton in Lexington, purchasing eight yearlings, led by Hip 166, a daughter of Girvin who brought $450,000 to top the filly market.

title_en: Angel of Kirk, Talleyrand Deliver on Promise
description: Angel of Kirk and Talleyrand, two smartly bred juveniles, are new BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects after posting first-out victories.

title_en: Sports Executive Maffei Keynote Speaker at Round Table
description: The Jockey Club today announced the agenda, including a list of speakers on timely topics, for its 2026 Round Table Conference on Matters Pertaining to Racing.

title_en: Monmouth Honors ABR's Tordjman With Handleman Award
description: Dan Tordjman, president of The Jockey Club's Media Ventures and America's Best Racing, has been named the 2026 Bill Handleman Award winner for outstanding coverage of last year's Haskell Stakes (G1), Monmouth Park announced today.

title_en: Vekoma Colt Sells for $600K at Fasig-Tipton July Sale
description: Kristian Villante of Legion Bloodstock signs July 14 for the purchase of a chestnut son of Vekoma for $600,000 during The July Sale at Fasig-Tipton in Lexington. Sold as Hip 135, the colt took the lead as the top seller of the session.

title_en: Ombudsman Drives Strong Demand on Darley Platform
description: A breeding right to Godolphin's Ombudsman, the world's equal highest rated racehorse, proved popular on Darley's bespoke "Winning Bid" platform July 13, selling to an unnamed buyer.

title_en: FanDuel TV Returns For Del Mar Amid Programming Shift
description: FanDuel TV will return to Del Mar with on-site coverage of its meet, which begins July 17. The summer season at Del Mar continues through Labor Day Sept. 7.

title_en: Magnitude Stays Solidly Atop NTRA Thoroughbred Poll
description: Undefeated so far this year, Magnitude is being aimed next for the Whitney Stakes (G1), where he could face Nysos.

title_en: NYRA Using Waigr to Put AI Technology in Patrons' Hands
description: The New York Racing Association has been a leader in establishing guardrails to prevent CAWs from flooding mutuel pools. Now NYRA is taking another big step toward leveling the wagering playing field for its wagering customers by teaming with Waigr.

title_en: Talk to Me Jimmy Wins Stretch Battle in New York Derby
description: Grade 3-placed Talk to Me Jimmy prevails in a deep-stretch battle July 13 at Finger Lakes Gaming & Racetrack to capture the $164,000 New York Derby by 1 1/4 lengths over Sculcos Folly in a 1 1/16-mile test for New York-breds.

title_en: BH Monday: Napoleon Solo Returns in Haskell
description: On the July 13 episode of BloodHorse Monday: Napoleon Solo's trainer, Chad Summers, and Monmouth Park CEO and chairman Dennis Drazin discuss the July 18 Haskell Stakes (G1).

title_en: Delaware Park Cancels July 15 Card Due to Extreme Heat
description: Live racing is scheduled to resume Thursday, July 16.

title_en: Purse Enhancements, Incentives Added to Woodbine Racing
description: Ontario's Thoroughbred racing community announced July 13 significant purse enhancements for the 2026 Thoroughbred racing season at Woodbine Racetrack. In addition to higher purses, more money will be provided for breeders' awards.

title_en: Lone Star Park Sees Attendance, Handle Increase
description: Lone Star Park concluded its 30th Spring Thoroughbred racing season July 12, handling a daily average of $251,403 in live ontrack handle and a daily average of $170,053 in export handle over the 41-day meet; both increases over 2025 figures.

title_en: Jockeys' Guild Responds to Saratoga Distance Change
description: Following controversy around a late change in distance of Saratoga Race Course's fourth race July 12, the Jockeys' Guild has released a statement explaining its viewpoint.

title_en: Looking at Historical Trends of the Haskell Stakes
description: Some of the best 3-year-old Thoroughbreds in the country will battle in one of the marquee stakes in the division when they face off in the $1 million Haskell Stakes (G1) July 18 at Monmouth Park.

title_en: Four Star Sales Welcomes Hamilton to its Team
description: Four Star Sales has announced that Grace Hamilton has joined the company as bloodstock and sales coordinator. Hamilton joins Four Star following graduation from the internationally respected Godolphin Flying Start program.

title_en: Crazy Hot Mess Becomes First U.S. Winner for Pinehurst
description: Crazy Hot Mess became the first winner in the U.S. for Walmac Farm's Pinehurst when the 2-year-old filly broke her maiden on debut at Ellis Park to win a maiden special weight race by 1 1/4 lengths.

title_en: Thesecretadversary Gives Stack First European G1
description: Thesecretadversary proved himself worthy of mention among the best of the 3-year-old crop with a barnstorming all-the-way success in the Prix Jean Prat (G1) under Christophe Soumillon.

title_en: Liam's Law Becomes First Winner for Colonel Liam
description: Liam's Law became the first winner for Ocala Stud's multiple graded stakes winner and freshman sire Colonel Liam, after breaking his maiden July 12 in a maiden special weight race over the turf at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Taylor Made Offers Half Sister to Booked
description: First-crop sire Two Phil's will be represented by Hip 52, a half sister to recent Sanford Stakes (G3) winner Booked. The filly sells July 14 at Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale with potential to become her dam's third stakes performer.

title_en: Rattle N Roll Retired, Heads to Stud in Saudi Arabia
description: Grade 1 winner Rattle N Roll, an earner of nearly $4 million, has been retired from racing at age 7 because of bone bruising, and will begin a stallion career in Saudi Arabia in 2027, according to co-owner Mike Mackin of Lucky Seven Stable.

title_en: Classic Winners Headline July Sale's First-Crop Sires
description: Kentucky Derby (G1) winner Mage and Belmont Stakes (G1) winner Arcangelo are among 19 first-crop yearling sires represented in Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale being held July 14 in Lexington.

title_en: Juddmonte Stallion Zamindar Dies at 32
description: Smart racehorse and decorated stallion Zamindar has died at the age of 32, Juddmonte reported July 10.

title_en: MD Horse Breeders Association Board Selects Officers
description: The board of directors of the Maryland Horse Breeders Association reelects Richard Blue as president, Josh Pons as vice president, and Michael Horning as secretary/treasurer.

title_en: Ontario Thoroughbred Industry Gets $10M Boost
description: Ontario Racing announces that $10 million in new funding for the province's Thoroughbred racing industry will be used to invest in increased breeders' rewards, purses, and overall expansion of Ontario-bred racing opportunities.

title_en: Velvet Beretta Becomes Cyberknife's First Winner
description: The first-out winner captured a 5 1/2-furlong maiden special weight July 9 at Ellis Park by 3 lengths.

title_en: Country Life Farm to Stand Regally Bred Provenance
description: While injury limited on-track opportunities for grade 2-placed Provenance, the son of seven-time leading sire Into Mischief out of two-time champion Monomoy Girl will bring plenty of star power to Maryland when he stands at Country Life Farm in 2027.

title_en: When Sweet Azteca Runs, She's Very, Very Good
description: The daughter of Sharp Azteca has started 10 times in three years, but owns six graded stakes wins among those starts. The key to her female family's success could be in the daughters' mitochondrial DNA.

title_en: Broodmare Retirement Summit July 24 at Keeneland
description: Mareworthy Charities will host the Thoroughbred Broodmare Retirement Summit from 1-3 p.m. Friday, July 24, at Keeneland. The event will update the work to strengthen long-term outcomes for mares after their breeding careers end.

title_en: Scarlett Begonia, Antivenom Blossom at Ellis Park
description: Two nicely bred juveniles became BloodHorse Maiden Watch Hot Prospects with comfortable debut victories at Ellis Park. Antivenom, a son of Candy Ride, broke his maiden July 4 before the Life Is Good filly Scarlett Begonia followed suit July 6.

title_en: Englishman to Stud at Lane's End After Racing
description: Englishman recently won the Woody Stephens Stakes (G1) at Saratoga Race Course. He is expected to continue racing through 2027.

title_en: Greatest Joy is First Winner for Greatest Honour
description: The Divine Light Healing Ministries homebred filly wins her racing debut July 6 with trainer Kevin Rice at Presque Isle Downs by 1 1/4 lengths.

title_en: Researcher O'Casaigh Buys 90% Interest in Early Voting
description: Dr. Padraig O'Casaigh announced July 6 he bought the majority interest in Early Voting. He said proceeds from the stallion's stud fees will be used to provide financial assistance to veterinary school students.

title_en: Breeders’ Cup Changes New Foal Nomination Fee Structure; Will Be Based On Stud Fee
description: Beginning in 2027, the Breeders' Cup will offer a new structure for foal nomination fees to be based on the stallion's advertised fee, the group announced Wednesday.
